name: Rionorese

ancestors.notes: Sanabrian Leonese adstrate: the Sanabria comarca of Zamora province is geographically and linguistically adjacent to Rionor. Shared features include full betacism; the two differ on the predorsal sibilant, however — Sanabria keeps the interdental [θ] while Rio de Onor/Guadramil are seseante [s] (Vasconcelos EPM II p.54).

Old Portuguese adstrate: as Portuguese consolidated as the state language, the Leonese-speaking communities of Miranda, Rionor, and Guadramil faced intensifying pressure. Portuguese orthographic conventions (lh, nh, ã) penetrated the writing tradition.

notes: Rionorese (Rionorês), the nearly-extinct Asturleonese variety spoken in Rio de Onor (PT) / Rihonor de Castilla (ES), straddling the Portuguese-Spanish border in Trás-os-Montes. Fewer than 100 elderly speakers as of the early 2000s (Macias 2003). Grounded in Macias (2003) and Carvalho & Dias (1955). KEY PHONOLOGICAL FEATURES: (1) FULL BETACISM /v/ → /b/: No /v/ phoneme; ⟨v⟩ and ⟨b⟩ both surface as /b/ (with intervocalic fricative allophone [β]). Examples: *baliente* (valente), *ber* (ver), *habia* (havia), *bira* (vira), *bergonha* (vergonha), *benhir* (vir). Feature shared with the Transmontano falar (Macias 2003, p. 26, fn. 28; stated-explicitly). (2) AFFRICATE /tʃ/ WRITTEN ⟨tch⟩: *tchamar* (chamar), *escatchar* (partir), *matchada* (machada), *martchar-se* (ir-se embora) (Macias 2003, p. 26; stated-explicitly). ⟨ch⟩ (without t-) = /ʃ/ (Portuguese-type, not affricate). (3) DIPHTHONGS: Latin /e/ → ⟨ie⟩/⟨iê⟩ /je/: *piedra, pierna, prendie-lu, baliente, iera* (Macias 2003, p. 25; stated-explicitly). Latin /o/ → ⟨uô⟩ /wɔ/: *ruôdra* (roda) (Macias 2003, p. 26; stated-explicitly). Latin /o/ in other environments → ⟨uâ⟩ /wɐ/ (timbre semelhante ao -a fechado do português): *puârta, fuârte, muârtu* (Macias 2003, p. 26; stated-explicitly). (4) FINAL -o → /u/: systematic — *piquenu, muârtu, suxeitu* (Macias 2003, pp. 25–26). Encoded as positional rule. (5) SIBILANT VOICING MERGER ([z]/[s] neutralization): No voicing contrast; attributed to Leonese influence (Macias 2003, p. 27; stated-explicitly). The merged sibilant quality (apical vs. laminal) is NOT stated in the source — do NOT encode apical/laminal values without evidence, so the predorsal reflex is the UNMARKED [s]. (5b) SESEO — ⟨ç⟩/⟨c⟩+e,i AND ⟨z⟩ = [s], NOT the Castilian interdental [θ]: this NW-corner border falar merges the predorsal sibilant with the Galician [s] (Vasconcelos, EPM II, p. 54, on the neighbouring guadramilês), unlike the Sanabria continuum to the east (ast-x-sanabria) which keeps [θ]. An earlier draft encoded [θ] on the strength of the shared betacism with Sanabria, but that value was never positively attested for Rio de Onor/Guadramil; Vasconcelos's galego-merger is the positive evidence, and it is inherited by the Guadramil child. (6) [s] PALATALIZATION in certain words: *dixu* (disse), *dixes* (dizes) — ⟨x⟩ represents [ʃ] (Macias 2003, p. 27; stated-explicitly). LL-CONFLICT AND ADJUDICATION: The Sanabrese source (Frías-Conde, p. 4) claims initial L- → /ʎ/ for the entire Sanabrian zone including the sub-varieties at Rio de Onor. However, the Rionorese monograph (Macias 2003) is entirely SILENT on initial ll- palatalization — its l-examples show yod-outcomes (l yotizes and disappears: *al conseio* < o conselho, *un pouco mior* < um pouco melhor, p. 27) rather than retention of /ʎ/. RESOLUTION: ⟨ll⟩ = /ʎ/ is retained in this spec (inherited from the medieval parent via graphemes_base) because: (a) the Sanabrese source positively asserts it for the zone; (b) Macias's monograph does not deny it — the yod-outcomes she documents affect medial /l/ in specific clusters, not initial ⟨ll⟩. However, the conflict is recorded: initial /ʎ/ may have weakened or merged in Rionorese; the source is insufficient to confirm retention definitively. This should be treated as MEDIUM confidence only. A word lexicon is not bundled: register one with orthography2ipa.register_lexicon("ast-PT-x-rionor", <path|url|hf://…>).

sources.author: Macias, Dina Rodrigues
sources.title: Dialecto rionorês: Contributo para o seu estudo
sources.publisher: Instituto Politécnico de Bragança, Série Estudos 64
sources.notes: ISBN: 972-745-071-7. Primary source for Rionorese phonology. Published 2003 (accepted 1999). All Rionorese-specific claims are cited from this work.

sources.author: Carvalho, José Herculano de & Dias, Jorge
sources.title: O Falar de Rio de Onor
sources.notes: Foundational primary reference for Rionorese; primary reference throughout Macias (2003).

sources.author: Frías-Conde, Xavier
sources.title: El Habla de Sanabria
sources.publisher: CES Don Bosco (UCM); ISR Romania Minor
sources.notes: Claims initial L- > /ʎ/ for the Sanabrian zone including Rio de Onor sub-zone. Conflicts with Macias's silence — see LL-CONFLICT notes above. Year is approximate (undated document).

sources.author: Vasconcelos, J. Leite de
sources.title: Estudos de Philologia Mirandesa (Vol. II)
sources.notes: EPM II p. 54 describes the NW border falar of Guadramil (guadramilês), documenting that its ⟨ç⟩/⟨c⟩+e,i predorsal sibilant merges with the Galician [s] (seseo) rather than surfacing as the Castilian interdental [θ] kept by the Sanabria continuum to the east — the basis for the seseante [s] reflex in this spec and its Guadramil child.

location.notes: Rio de Onor (Bragança) — the single border village, straddling the frontier with Rihonor de Castilla, that the variety is spoken in. [Wikidata Q2119682 P625]
